water.name: Santa Ynez River and tributaries downstream from Bradbury Dam

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(99) — Santa Ynez River and tributaries downstream from Bradbury Dam (Santa Barbara Co.)
sources.quotedText: Santa Ynez River and tributaries downstream from Bradbury Dam (Santa Barbara Co.). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: Santa Ynez River Restoration | California Trout
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Bradbury Dam (forming Lake Cachuma) blocks upstream passage for the endangered Southern California steelhead DPS; the river and all tributaries downstream of the dam are closed to all fishing to protect remaining steelhead.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year on the Santa Ynez River and tributaries downstream of Bradbury Dam
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license (not applicable here since the water is closed to all fishing, but included for completeness).

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+ (statewide baseline; this water is closed to fishing)
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
